symptom: Seed glossary imported operator-personal vocabulary standard wholesale as canonical terms with aggressive forbidden-synonym blocks on ordinary English words. Result: 180+ violations in template's own prose on first run. Regex lint cannot distinguish citation from substitution. Fix: seed ships zero forbidden-synonym blocks; enforcement earns activation when an instance populates rules/canon.yaml with real drift evidence.

Synapse copies of ACW rules at ~/synapse/Rules/Procedures/ are stale; missing canon-governance.md, canon.yaml, canon-schema.yaml, vocabulary-lint.md, promotion-ritual.md that exist in ACW canonical. Agent loaded synapse copies as authoritative, made incorrect statements about ACW state in chat, corrected by operator.

gsg-copilot did not bootstrap from /Projects/acw/bootstrap/ (which has only a README). Instance grew its substrate parallel-evolution-style. Multiple decisions diverged from canonical without justification: CLAUDE.md instead of AGENTS.md, missing canon-governance system entirely, missing DEFERRED.md and promotion-ritual.md. No scaffolding mechanism exposes ACW canonical to new instances.

Agent skipped Phase 2 in initial dogfood, citing concern about overwriting already-distributed substrate. Operator surfaced this as a process gap (turn 37). Resolution: future dogfoods on fresh clones run full five-phase by default; sandboxes that match post-distribution state run Phase 1 only and explicitly note Phase 2 was skipped.

ACW ships bookend-arc skills at acw/skills/ via template_layer, but Claude Code does not auto-discover them. Fresh operator on a fresh ACW clone fires /resume-session and nothing happens. Manual junction setup at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/ required. Future scaffold-instance.py could automate junction creation; deferred to OQ-ACW-006 pending second-instance evidence.

Agent reading rules/skill-format.md applied the strict-voice four-test ('same invariant workflow, same reasoning steps, same order') to flag /acw-session start|end as a rule violation, even though the permissive-voice command-count ladder explicitly carved out object-centered workbenches at 10+ commands. The two voices contradicted each other for the same pattern. Operator pointed at Impeccable (the format's own cited precedent) to correct the false-flag. Resolution: tightened skill-format in v0.4.0 with three edits — reframed test 1 as 'same shared spine,' split strongest-version rule by orientation, scoped deltas-are-configuration to spine only. The rule now self-validates Impeccable-shape patterns.

First /acw-instance audit dogfood against _Command exposed five bugs in v0.4.0: (1) hard-stop scan counted only decisions/ and rules/ files; missed root-level organic substrate (briefings/, runbooks/, integrations/, etc.) which is exactly the case the threshold was designed to catch. (2) Mode B walk produced static report with proposed routings ('Likely [s]', 'Possibly [b]') instead of interactive prompts; nothing landed in _buffer/. (3) Default routing was [s] instance-specific; should be 'ask, don't guess.' (4) Skills audit not part of verb spine — found 6 skills under _Command/skills/ but didn't validate frontmatter. (5) Absorption flow gated on workspace registration (cross_repo_writes block missing); should work pre-adoption. All five fixed in v0.5.0; spec tightened so future verb implementations can't drift.

v0.4.0-era audit verdicts (and earlier substrate-categorization choices) leaned conservative — when in doubt, classified patterns as 'instance-specific' rather than 'absorption candidate.' _Command audit flagged briefings/, runbooks/, integrations/ all as 'Likely [s] instance-specific.' Operator pushback corrected: all three are universal patterns; only the content varies by workspace type. Three substrate categories absorbed in v0.5.0. Lesson: distinguish pattern shape (universal or not) from pattern content (varies by type); 'shape vs content' is the right discriminator for absorption decisions.
